message:
fix(net): hold lock across driver.register() in TCP and Unix split streams to prevent EEXIST race

When both halves of a split TCP or Unix stream concurrently entered
ensure_registration()/register_interest(), each could observe no
existing registration and call driver.register() simultaneously. The
second call would fail with EEXIST from epoll_ctl(ADD) because the
lock was dropped before the registration call.

TCP split (src/net/tcp/split.rs):
- Removed the premature drop(guard) before driver.register()
- Store the registration directly into guard.registration
- Added explicit drop(guard) after the match to clarify lifetime
- Built SourceExclusiveReactor test harness that enforces single-
  registration semantics (returns EEXIST on duplicate fd or token)
- Added split_register_interest_serializes_fresh_registration test:
  two threads race through a Barrier to register simultaneously;
  asserts exactly 1 register() call and 1 modify() call

Unix split (src/net/unix/split.rs):
- Applied the same lock-holding pattern: store registration while
  guard is alive, drop guard after match completes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
